claim: 第一階段研究不應直接寫總報告，而應先建立 source map、digest、claim table 與 open questions。
USDT Q1 2026 reserve report is an ISAE 3000R assurance report limited to a point in time, not full financial statements.
evidence: BDO report and financial figures/reserves report scope statements

claim: Tether International report states March 31 2026 reserves exceeded fiat-denominated token liabilities by approximately US$8.232bn.
evidence: Financial figures table and management report

claim: PYUSD is issued by Paxos Trust Company, N.A. and backed by U.S. dollar deposits, U.S. Treasuries and similar cash equivalents.
PayPal announced PYUSD availability across 70 markets and framed it as faster/lower-cost global commerce infrastructure.
USDG is issued by Paxos Digital Singapore Pte. Ltd. and subject to MAS oversight; it is redeemable 1:1 for USD from Paxos.
notes: Need terms for direct redeemer scope

claim: RLUSD is issued by Standard Custody & Trust Company, LLC, a New York limited purpose trust company and Ripple Labs subsidiary.
RLUSD reserve criteria are derived from NYDFS June 8 2022 guidance and include segregated reserves and permitted reserve assets.
FDUSD March 2026 report is a limited assurance engagement under ISAE 3000 Revised, not reasonable assurance.
FDUSD reserve accounts report identifies FD121 (BVI) Limited, multi-chain FDUSD supply, and reserve accounts at least equal to outstanding supply.
GUSD is a New York trust company-issued, 1:1 USD-pegged ERC-20 token, created and redeemed on Gemini platform.
GUSD February 2026 reserves report examines Gemini Trust Company compliance with NYDFS stablecoin guidance and states reserves fully back GUSD.
evidence: Independent accountant report and management assertion

claim: BIS AER 2025 assesses stablecoins against singleness, elasticity and integrity and concludes they fall short as mainstay of money.
BIS WP 1270 estimates a 2-standard deviation stablecoin inflow lowers 3-month T-bill yields by roughly 2.5-3.5 bps, larger in bill scarcity.
ECB WP 3199 argues stablecoin adoption can shift funds from retail deposits to digital assets, increasing wholesale funding reliance and weakening monetary policy predictability.
FSB 2025 review finds GSC regulatory implementation remains fragmented and uneven, with gaps in redemption, custody, reserves, capital buffers and resolution planning.
CPMI 2016 describes correspondent banking as access to financial services across jurisdictions and a backbone of cross-border payments, but notes relationship reduction and concentration.
CPMI 2018 finds cross-border retail payments are perceived as slower, costlier and more opaque, and that many PSPs use correspondent banks for settlement and FX.
OCC proposed timely redemption rule may not exceed two business days, with extension to seven calendar days if 24h redemption demand exceeds 10% of outstanding issuance.
Treasury principles state state-level regimes must be substantially similar to federal framework and aligned on reserve assets, redemption and rehypothecation.
CBC argues stablecoins may need to be incorporated into monetary statistics/monitoring if they become broadly used for payments, wages, savings or cross-border flows.
CBC notes USD stablecoins could create a parallel on-chain USD inflow/outflow channel and affect FX monitoring if not subject to existing reporting controls.
Anchorage comment letter says ADB issues USAT, USDGO and USDtb, and expects to begin issuing UDSPT with Western Union as brand partner.
USDG whitepaper states USDG can settle in minutes on Ethereum/approved networks versus potential days for fiat movements.
FSB identifies varied redemption-fee and redemption-period approaches across jurisdictions as potential run-risk and cross-border reserve management issue.
